source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design the primary lifting-arm structural yoke of a VTOL heavy-lift UAS defense-lift benchmark as a multi-part titanium assembly consisting of the main yoke body (beam), two press-fit/bolted top attachment lug bushings (to fuselage carry-through), one central load-pin bushing at the bottom (to payload hook), and two attachment bolt sets (fuselage side + payload pin side). Targeting a payload-to-empty-weight ratio of >= 4.0 with VTOL-only operation. Target configuration: empty weight 100 kg, maximum payload 450 kg (ratio 4.5), MTOW 600 kg (including 50 kg fuel/battery). Lifting yoke is the structural beam that connects the vehicle fuselage to the payload hook via a central load pin at 1.0 m below the vehicle CG. The yoke must transmit 4410 N (450 kg x 9.81 m/s^2) vertical plus dynamic pendulum loads into the fuselage frame. Material: Titanium 6Al-4V STOA (Young's modulus 113.8 GPa, Poisson 0.342, yield 860 MPa, ultimate 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Yoke envelope 800 mm width x 300 mm depth x 80 mm thickness, machined from billet with two 80 mm OD top attachment lugs (to fuselage carry-through) and one 50 mm central load pin bore at the bottom (to payload hook). Load cases per heavy-lift trial conditions: (LC1) static hover with max payload: 4410 N vertical on central load pin with top lugs reacted; (LC2) pendulum oscillation: 450 kg payload swinging +/- 20 deg at 0.7 Hz (natural period of 1 m tether), generating horizontal component up to 1560 N in both directions; (LC3) gust arrest: step gust encounter producing 3 g vertical at vehicle = 13,230 N through yoke for 200 ms; (LC4) snatch pick-up: zero-velocity pick-up of a 450 kg payload at zero-to-4 m/s in 500 ms = 3600 N additional dynamic = 8010 N total peak; (LC5) one-hook failure: asymmetric load 4410 N concentrated at one top lug (single-attachment fail-safe case); (LC6) fatigue: 5000 missions x (100 g cycle per mission) = 5x10^5 cycles of 0-4410 N. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises across LC1-LC5 <= 633 MPa (ultimate 950 / 1.5); (R2) at limit loads LC1-LC4, max stress <= 748 MPa (yield 860 / 1.15); (R3) pin-bore ovalization under LC1 <= 0.1 mm; (R4) fatigue alternating stress at fillet <= 160 MPa (Ti-6Al-4V Kt=3 endurance at 5x10^5); (R5) yoke mass <= 18 kg (contribution to 100 kg empty); (R6) first lateral bending mode of loaded yoke above 3 Hz (well above 0.7 Hz pendulum to avoid coupling). Verification is performed on the assembled yoke body + top-lug bushings + load-pin bushing multi-body model with representative press-fit + bolted interface stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, fatigue summary, modal, mass report.
short_prompt:
Design the primary lifting-arm structural yoke of a VTOL heavy-lift UAS defense-lift benchmark as a multi-part titanium assembly (yoke beam + two top-lug bushings + central load-pin bushing + bolt sets).

Targeting payload-to-empty ratio >= 4.0: empty 100 kg, max payload 450 kg (ratio 4.5), MTOW 600 kg (incl. 50 kg fuel/battery). Yoke connects fuselage to payload hook via a central load pin 1.0 m below vehicle CG, transmitting 4410 N vertical plus dynamic pendulum loads. Material Titanium 6Al-4V STOA (E 113.8 GPa, Poisson 0.342, yield 860 MPa, ultimate 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Envelope 800 mm W x 300 mm D x 80 mm thick, two 80 mm OD top attachment lugs and one 50 mm central load pin bore.

Load cases.
- LC1 static hover with max payload: 4410 N vertical at central load pin.
- LC2 pendulum: 450 kg at +/- 20 deg at 0.7 Hz producing up to 1560 N horizontal.
- LC3 gust arrest: 3 g vertical step = 13,230 N through yoke for 200 ms.
- LC4 snatch pick-up: zero-to-4 m/s in 500 ms adds 3600 N dynamic (8010 N peak).
- LC5 one-hook failure: asymmetric 4410 N at one top lug.
- LC6 fatigue: 5x10^5 cycles of 0-4410 N.

Pin-bore ovalization under LC1 <= 0.1 mm; first lateral bending mode of loaded yoke > 3 Hz.

prompt.title: Heavy-lift UAS lifting-arm yoke (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Ti-6Al-4V yoke body + 2 top-lug bushings + central load-pin bushing + 2 bolt sets transmitting 450 kg payload.

parts.name: yoke_body
parts.role: main titanium beam machined from billet, 800 mm x 300 mm x 80 mm envelope

parts.name: top_attachment_lug_bushing
parts.role: press-fit bushing at each of two 80 mm OD top lugs interfacing the fuselage carry-through pin

parts.name: central_load_pin_bushing
parts.role: bushing at the 50 mm central load-pin bore interfacing the payload hook pin

parts.name: top_attachment_bolt_set
parts.role: bolt set tying the yoke body's top lugs into the fuselage carry-through structure

parts.name: payload_pin_retention_set
parts.role: retention hardware holding the central load pin in the payload-side bushing

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 800 mm x 300 mm x 80 mm yoke body
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +X lateral (two top lugs at +/-X), -Z downward to central payload pin

boundary_conditions.interfaces: top-lug bushings press-fit + bolted into yoke body; central load-pin bushing press-fit into bore; top attachment bolt set preloads lugs into fuselage carry-through with representative bolt stiffness
boundary_conditions.support: two top-lug pins fixed to fuselage carry-through in LC1-LC4; single top-lug fixed in LC5

prompt.objective: minimize yoke mass under 18 kg while passing yield, ultimate, ovalization, fatigue, and modal gates

interfaces.load_path: top-lug bushing press-fit + bolted into yoke body lug bore
central load-pin bushing press-fit into yoke body bore
top attachment bolt set preloading lugs into fuselage carry-through
payload pin retention hardware holding central load pin in bushing

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Heavy-lift yokes are a titanium body with press-fit lug and pin bushings plus top attachment bolt set; pin-bore ovalization and one-hook-failure gates explicitly cross the bushing/bolt interfaces and must be modelled as a multi-body assembly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Bushing material and retention hardware detail are speculative; yoke material, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
